---
title: "Research Question 2 (USGS vs. Thesis)"
format: docx
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(Rmisc)
library(rstatix)
library(tidyr)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(stringr)
#install.packages("readxl")
library(readxl)
#install.packages("lubridate")
#install.packages("lmerTest")
library(lmerTest)
#install.packages("ggeffects")
library(ggeffects)
library(ggforce)

knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = 'C:/Users/savan/OneDrive - San Francisco State University/ProjectData/GitHub/DepositionSFB/data')
```

```{r}


```

```{r}

thesis_rq2 <- usgs_thesis_M1_m6_no150 %>%
  filter(!Site %in% c("Buck'sLanding", "GiantMarsh"))

thesis_rq2 <- thesis_rq2 %>% select(-marsh_edge_type, -marsh_orientation,-Study.Dataset)

# Verify they're gone
unique(thesis_rq2$Site)
levels(thesis_rq2$Site)

str(thesis_rq2)
nrow(thesis_rq2) 
```

```{r, statistics}

shapiro.test(thesis_rq2$Flux)

qqnorm(thesis_rq2$Flux)

qqline(thesis_rq2$Flux)



```

```{r}

usgsthesis_model<- lmer(Flux ~ Year + Study.Phase + Year*Study.Phase + (1|Season) + (1|Site), data = thesis_rq2)
summary(usgsthesis_model)
```

```{r}
usgsthesis_model2<- lmer(Flux ~ Site + (1|Year) + (1|Season), data = thesis_rq2) 
summary(usgsthesis_model2)
```

```{r}


# Get estimated marginal means
emm <- emmeans(usgsthesis_model, ~ Study.Phase * Year)
emm_df <- as.data.frame(emm)

ggplot(emm_df, aes(x = Study.Phase, y = emmean, color = Year, group = Year)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower.CL, ymax = upper.CL), 
                width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(0.3)) +
  labs(title = "Estimated Marginal Means by Study Phase and Year",
       y = "log(Flux)", x = "Study Phase") +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(thesis_rq2, aes(x = Site, y = Flux, fill = as.factor(Year))) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA) +  # remove outlier dots from boxplot
  geom_jitter(aes(color = as.factor(Year)), 
              alpha = 0.4,        # more transparent
              size = 0.8,         # smaller points
              width = 0.2) +      # less horizontal spread
  labs(title = str_wrap("Research Question 2: Is Sediment Deposition Consistent Between Years at Corte Madera Marsh and San Pablo NWR?", width = 40),
       y = "Log of Average Flux (g/m^3/day)", 
       x = "Site",
       fill = "Year", color = "Year") +
scale_y_log10() + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme( 
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13),      # match x axis
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13),        # match x axis
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 13),        
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 13),      
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),        
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13),       
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )
```

```{r}

ggplot(usgsthesiscomp, aes(x = Site, y = log(Flux), fill = as.factor(Tran), colour = as.factor(Tran))) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_sina(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Site",
    y = "Log of Average Flux (g/cm³)",
    fill = "Transect",
    colour = "Transect"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r, last hooraah}

# Check column names of each dataframe
colnames(sed_thesis)
colnames(sed_usgs)

# Check dimensions
ncol(sed_thesis)
ncol(sed_usgs)

# Find columns in df1 that are NOT in df2
setdiff(colnames(sed_thesis), colnames(sed_usgs))

# Find columns in df2 that are NOT in df1
setdiff(colnames(sed_thesis), colnames(sed_usgs))

hoorah_df <- bind_rows(sed_thesis, sed_usgs)

# Check it worked
nrow(sed_thesis) + nrow(sed_usgs)  # should equal nrow(combined_df)
colnames(hoorah_df)



```
